prompt: The code always prints "pass" regardless of the score. What is the bug?
choices: There is a semicolon after if (score > 60) making the if-body an empty statement; println always executes
System.out.println must be inside curly braces to be part of an if
int score should be declared as final to be used in a condition
explanation: The semicolon after if (score > 60); creates an empty if-body. The println that follows is not part of the if — it always runs. Fix: remove the semicolon, or use braces: if (score > 60) { System.out.println("pass"); }
